title: Changes to the Register of SMSF messaging providers
description: Find out about the upcoming changes to SMSF messaging providers and how they may impact your fund.
keywords: Changes, to, the, Register, of, SMSF, self-managed, super, funds, messaging, providers, Australia, Post, Colladium, Gateway, SuperChoice, Electronic, ESA, SuperStream, Payday, 30, June, 2026, tax

content_blocks.text: There are a number of changes to the register of SMSF messaging providers.

On 23 March 2026 , Colladium SMSF Gateway External Link commenced as a SMSF messaging provider offering a contributions‑only Electronic Service Addresses (ESA) service. This gives self-managed super funds (SMSFs) another compliant option for receiving SuperStream contributions.

Also from 5 May 2026 SuperChoice External Link will cease providing services as a SMSF messaging provider. Australia Post External Link has also indicated it will end their services altogether on 30 June 2026.

SMSFs currently using SuperChoice External Link or Australia Post External Link as their messaging providers will need to take action ahead of the announced exit dates for these services. Failing to take action could result in delays or issues with contributions being paid correctly.

Your SMSF can only have one ESA address recorded with us. This means if you require both contributions and rollovers or release authorities, then you need to select a provider that offers both services.

It’s important to note contributions‑only ESAs are designed specifically for receiving employer contributions and may not support other messaging or payment functions. Trustees should assess whether this type of service aligns with their fund’s needs and seek advice if they are unsure.
If you have questions about how these changes may impact your SMSF, speak with your tax agent or administrator.
Go to our Register of SMSF messaging providers to learn more.
Looking for the latest news for SMSFs? – You can stay up to date by visiting our SMSF newsroom and subscribing External Link to our monthly SMSF newsletter.

content_text:
There are a number of changes to the register of SMSF messaging providers.
On 23 March 2026 , Colladium SMSF Gateway External Link commenced as a SMSF messaging provider offering a contributions‑only Electronic Service Addresses (ESA) service. This gives self-managed super funds (SMSFs) another compliant option for receiving SuperStream contributions.
Also from 5 May 2026 SuperChoice External Link will cease providing services as a SMSF messaging provider. Australia Post External Link has also indicated it will end their services altogether on 30 June 2026.
SMSFs currently using SuperChoice External Link or Australia Post External Link as their messaging providers will need to take action ahead of the announced exit dates for these services. Failing to take action could result in delays or issues with contributions being paid correctly.
Your SMSF can only have one ESA address recorded with us. This means if you require both contributions and rollovers or release authorities, then you need to select a provider that offers both services.
It’s important to note contributions‑only ESAs are designed specifically for receiving employer contributions and may not support other messaging or payment functions. Trustees should assess whether this type of service aligns with their fund’s needs and seek advice if they are unsure.
If you have questions about how these changes may impact your SMSF, speak with your tax agent or administrator.
Go to our Register of SMSF messaging providers to learn more.
Looking for the latest news for SMSFs? – You can stay up to date by visiting our SMSF newsroom and subscribing External Link to our monthly SMSF newsletter.
